cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question. The user is asking about the World Bank's 1974 study on economic growth in some backward countries and what that growth brought. The options are A to D. First, I'll need to refer back to the passage provided. The relevant part is in the third paragraph. It says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So the key point here is that the study found that despite rapid growth over many years, the benefit was minimal for a third of the population. The question asks what the economic growth brought according to this study. Looking at the options: A) benefit to a third of their population B) benefit to two thirds of their population C) little benefit to their people D) no benefit at all to their people The passage says "little benefit to a third of their population," which directly matches option A. But wait, let me check again. The wording is important. The study says that the growth was "of little benefit to a third of their population."
